Request reprint permissions here.If China were to seize control of Taiwan by force, it would be a disaster, not only for Taiwan but also for the United States. A nearly $1 trillion economy would leave the free-market system and be incorporated into China’s state-directed, mercantilist one. A vibrant democracy nurtured and defended by the United States for many years would be snuffed out. American power and influence would be gravely diminished in East Asia, and China would become the region’s dominant power. Other governments there would be pressured to accommodate China’s political, economic, and even territorial demands. Beijing would certainly insist

DENNIS BLAIR was Commander in Chief of U.S. Pacific Command from 1999 to 2002 and Director of National Intelligence from 2009 to 2010. From 2003 to 2007, he was Senior Mentor for Taiwan’s annual self-defense exercise. More by Dennis Blair
